metrics.narrative: Business problem identified but not quantified. Buyer stated they need procurement and contract lifecycle management capabilities for Spend and CLM (Next_Step 2026-03-06 'Spend & CLM main focus'), expanding scope to include analytics and intake (ANA/Intake also shown in demo 2026-03-31). Leandro stated 'we need sense of price to create business case' (Next_Step 2026-03-31), indicating they are building an internal justification tied to cost but have not articulated quantified savings, cycle-time reduction, headcount impact, or error-rate improvement. Zycus offered to co-author a business case with quantified ROI (cycle-time, savings, productivity) on the 2026-03-31 demo call, but no ROI has been delivered or validated with the buyer as of 2026-07-07. Products in scope (Products__c): iContract, iSave, Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA. No Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, or Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c fields populated per SOQL Q3. Gap: no quantified value case confirmed with the buyer or economic buyer; business case is buyer's gate to budget approval but remains unbuilt 98 days after demo.

champion.narrative: Leandro Damasio is the identified champion but strength is developing and at risk of cooling. Role: Sr Director Strategic Sourcing (inferred from task contacts and Next_Step activity ownership). Evidence of advocacy: Leandro drives all evaluation activity from discovery (Next_Step 2026-02-27 'meeting tuesday for discovery') through demo scheduling (2026-03-06, 2026-03-12, 2026-03-16), demo execution (2026-03-31), and commercial engagement (2026-04-15 received commercial structure, 2026-04-22 asked questions, 2026-05-04 committed to follow up with commercial inputs). He has not introduced Zycus to the economic buyer, has not provided competitive intelligence (no competitor named), and has not delivered on his commitment to return commercial input sheet (requested 2026-05-12, overdue 56 days as of 2026-07-07). Access to EB/DM: unknown; no evidence Leandro has connected Zycus to Jon Gappa (SVP Sourcing, attended demo but no direct follow-up) or to the unmapped budget owner. Current engagement: cooling. Last buyer email from Leandro 2026-05-04 'we are busy, will follow up with commercial inputs this week', but inputs never arrived. Buyer OOO until late June (Next_Step 2026-06-10). LastActivityDate 2026-07-01 shows recent rep touch but no new buyer response as of 2026-07-07, 64 days since last buyer contact. Risk: Leandro may be deprioritizing Zycus, exploring other vendors more actively, or project slipping down priority list. Champion strength: developing but passive; has not mobilized internal support, has not delivered on commitments, and has gone quiet May-June. Next move: re-engage Leandro immediately post-OOO window (late June has passed), confirm project status and commercial-input timeline, and ask for introduction to the budget owner to co-present business case.

competition.narrative: Multi-vendor RFP field confirmed but no competitor named. Buyer stated 'evaluating multiple suppliers then going to RFP' (Next_Step 2026-03-06) and 'we will probably go to formal RFP' (2026-03-31), indicating Zycus is one of several vendors being considered. No competitor name provided in Next_Step history, no Competitors__c or Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c populated per SOQL Q3, no competitive intel in tasks or calls. Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Moved_To__c, Why_not_Zycus__c all null per SOQL Q3. Blind competitive position: we do not know if competitor has incumbent advantage (e.g. existing Workday partnership, legal-CLM install-base at Cinemark, prior relationship with Jon Gappa or Leandro), preferred pricing, or inside track on RFP criteria. Risk: RFP will commoditize evaluation and drive price competition; if competitor underbids Zycus or has stronger incumbent/relationship positioning, buyer may favor them even with weaker product fit. Zycus differentiation: integrated Spend+CLM+AI/Merlin platform (Products__c: iContract, iSave, Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) positions against point-solution competitors, but differentiation value not yet locked in RFP criteria (criteria unknown, RFP template offered but not confirmed delivered or adopted). How we win: (1) lock RFP criteria weighting toward integrated Spend+CLM and AI/Merlin (Zycus strengths) before RFP issuance, (2) map and engage economic buyer to position ROI and executive sponsorship before competitor does, (3) quantify business case to justify Zycus price vs lower-cost competitors. Next move: extract competitive intel from Leandro (who else is being evaluated, any incumbent, any preferred vendor) and deliver RFP template weighted to Zycus strengths before RFP goes out.

identify_pain.narrative: Operational pain identified but not quantified or tied to a pain owner. Buyer scope indicates need for procurement spend management and contract lifecycle management (Next_Step 2026-03-06 'Spend & CLM main focus'), with expansion to analytics and intake automation (Products__c: iContract, iSave, Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA). Leandro stated 'we need sense of price to create business case' (Next_Step 2026-03-31), implying the pain is significant enough to require budget justification but not yet articulated in terms of cost, cycle time, error rate, or headcount. No Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, or What_if_this_is_not_done__c populated per SOQL Q3. No call transcripts available to extract verbatim pain statements. Inferred pain: manual or fragmented procurement and contract processes, lack of spend visibility and analytics, no AI-driven intake or negotiation automation (based on product scope Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA). Pain owner: likely Leandro Damasio (Sr Director Strategic Sourcing, drives evaluation) and Jon Gappa (SVP Sourcing, attended demo), but no explicit pain-owner statement recorded. Buyer is early exploratory ('just beginning, trying to understand the market' 2026-03-31), so pain articulation may still be developing. Gap: no quantified impact (savings target, cycle-time reduction, error-rate improvement), no compelling event forcing timeline, no 'what if this is not done' consequence stated. Next move: on next call, ask Leandro to quantify the pain (current manual hours, error rate, contract backlog, savings opportunity) and identify the executive sponsor who owns the pain and will approve budget to fix it.

No MSA/SOW, legal review, procurement channel, or InfoSec review mentioned in Next_Step history (2026-02-27 to 2026-07-07) or populated in Salesforce fields (SOQL Q3: no paper-process-related fields available in this org). No Current_Contract_Expiration__c populated per SOQL Q2, so no forcing-function contract deadline. No SI/partner involvement mentioned. Deal is pre-commercial-close: still in vendor evaluation, pricing not finalized, RFP not issued, so paper process has not been scoped. Given enterprise deal size ($150k, Products scope iContract+iSave+Merlin+ANA) and buyer profile (Cinemark Theatres, large enterprise), expect standard MSA/SOW, legal redlining, InfoSec/data-privacy review (especially for AI/Merlin tenant isolation), and potentially ERP/procurement-system integration scoping. Gap: no visibility into contracting timeline, legal review requirements, InfoSec gate, or procurement-channel constraints. Next step to create visibility: ask Leandro on next call what contracting and approval steps follow vendor selection, and whether InfoSec/legal will review before or after RFP selection.

cro_panel.intro: A strong, well-attended discovery-to-demo run in March established real pain-point fit with a 7-person committee, but the deal has since cooled; no confirmed buyer reply since 4 May 2026, an unmapped economic buyer, and an unnamed but confirmed multi-vendor RFP process ahead

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: No economic buyer mapped or engaged; budget approval undefined.
Buyer is price-shopping to build business case and will run formal RFP, creating price-comparison risk.

blocks.title: Deal momentum

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Cinemark confirmed on 6 Mar 2026 and again on 31 Mar 2026 that it is evaluating multiple suppliers ahead of a formal RFP, but no rival has ever been named in Salesforce or on any call.

bullets.full: Multi-vendor RFP creates loss risk via competitor preferred-positioning or RFP-scoring-criteria mismatch. Buyer stated 'evaluating multiple suppliers then going to RFP' (Next_Step 2026-03-06) and 'we will probably go to formal RFP' (2026-03-31). No competitor named, so we are blind to competitive positioning. If competitor has incumbent advantage (e.g. existing Workday partnership, legal-CLM install-base at Cinemark, or prior relationship with Jon Gappa/Leandro), they may have inside track on RFP criteria. If RFP scoring weights price or legal-CLM features higher than AI/procurement-analytics (Zycus strengths), we lose despite product fit. Buyer is early exploratory ('just beginning, trying to understand market', 2026-03-31) and has time ('Q3 or Q1 2027'), so no urgency to sole-source Zycus — RFP will run full multi-vendor cycle. Risk: RFP commoditizes evaluation and buyer selects competitor on price, incumbent advantage, or criteria weighting we didn't influence.
bullets.text: Multi-vendor RFP creates loss risk via competitor preferred-positioning or RFP-scoring-criteria mismatch.

bullets.full: Close date 2026-07-31 (24 days out) is not credible. Buyer stated 'ideally Q3 2026 implementation, but could be Q1 2027 depending on many factors; we have time' (2026-03-31) — this is a loose, buyer-conditional timeline, not a locked close. Remaining steps: issue formal RFP (intent stated 2026-03-31, not yet done), complete multi-vendor evaluation, select vendor, build and approve business case with economic buyer, negotiate commercials, execute contract (MSA/legal/InfoSec review). 24 days insufficient for this path. Buyer engagement cooling May-June: last buyer email 2026-05-04 (64 days ago), buyer OOO until late June (2026-06-10 Next_Step), no new contact as of 2026-07-07. Realistic close Q4 2026 or Q1 2027 per buyer's own statement. Risk: deal slips beyond current fiscal planning window or buyer deprioritizes if timeline stretches into 2027.
bullets.text: Close date 31 Jul 2026 (24 days out) is not credible.

items.detail: No economic buyer mapped or engaged; budget approval undefined. Leandro (champion, Sr Director) must 'present business case to the organization' (2026-03-31) but the approver (CFO/CPO/budget owner) is unknown. No budget figure stated, no Who_will_approve_budget__c field populated. Buyer is building business case to justify spend ('need sense of price to create business case', Leandro 2026-03-31) but has not secured funding. Initial SaaS pricing shared 2026-05-20 but buyer has not confirmed budget fit. Risk: EB may reject business case, deprioritize project, or approve lower budget than Zycus price, forcing price concession or loss. Buyer stated 'Q3 2026 ideal but could be Q1 2027 depending on factors' — timeline is budget-dependent, not locked.

Buyer is price-shopping to build business case and will run formal RFP, creating price-comparison risk. Initial SaaS pricing shared 2026-05-20 (Next_Step) but buyer requested commercial input sheet 2026-05-12 and has not provided inputs or confirmed pricing fit. Buyer stated 'we need sense of price to create business case' (2026-03-31) — price is a gate to approval, not yet cleared. Multi-vendor RFP ('evaluating multiple suppliers') will commoditize evaluation and drive price competition. If competitor underbids Zycus, buyer may favor them even with weaker product fit. Buyer accepted Zycus business-case support offer (ROI modeling to justify price), but no quantified ROI delivered yet. Risk: Zycus loses on price in RFP or buyer cannot justify budget at Zycus price-point.

Multi-vendor RFP creates loss risk via competitor preferred-positioning or RFP-scoring-criteria mismatch. Buyer stated 'evaluating multiple suppliers then going to RFP' (Next_Step 2026-03-06) and 'we will probably go to formal RFP' (2026-03-31). No competitor named, so we are blind to competitive positioning. If competitor has incumbent advantage (e.g. existing Workday partnership, legal-CLM install-base at Cinemark, or prior relationship with Jon Gappa/Leandro), they may have inside track on RFP criteria. If RFP scoring weights price or legal-CLM features higher than AI/procurement-analytics (Zycus strengths), we lose despite product fit. Buyer is early exploratory ('just beginning, trying to understand market', 2026-03-31) and has time ('Q3 or Q1 2027'), so no urgency to sole-source Zycus — RFP will run full multi-vendor cycle. Risk: RFP commoditizes evaluation and buyer selects competitor on price, incumbent advantage, or criteria weighting we didn't influence.

Close date 2026-07-31 (24 days out) is not credible. Buyer stated 'ideally Q3 2026 implementation, but could be Q1 2027 depending on many factors; we have time' (2026-03-31) — this is a loose, buyer-conditional timeline, not a locked close. Remaining steps: issue formal RFP (intent stated 2026-03-31, not yet done), complete multi-vendor evaluation, select vendor, build and approve business case with economic buyer, negotiate commercials, execute contract (MSA/legal/InfoSec review). 24 days insufficient for this path. Buyer engagement cooling May-June: last buyer email 2026-05-04 (64 days ago), buyer OOO until late June (2026-06-10 Next_Step), no new contact as of 2026-07-07. Realistic close Q4 2026 or Q1 2027 per buyer's own statement. Risk: deal slips beyond current fiscal planning window or buyer deprioritizes if timeline stretches into 2027.

Deliver it this week so Leandro has ammo for internal approval and the business case anchors the RFP to Zycus strengths.

hard.ais_why:
• The March 31 2026 demo confirmed alignment on the AI value proposition: consolidated spend analytics, AI-driven CLM and autonomous negotiation were validated against Cinemark's pain points. The buyer acknowledged the AI fit but IT raised a governance constraint requiring AI models to run in a private tenant with no Cinemark data leaving that environment. That is sophisticated AI engagement with guardrails.
• Buyer questions on AutoClass AI classification, Merlin intake orchestration via Teams, MS Word InstaReview for clause-level risk, and Anna autonomous negotiation for tail-spend were active but reactive to the vendor flow. SVP Jon Gappa was in the room with Senior Directors Damasio and Flowers, with the close framed as comparing Zycus against other solutions rather than committing to AI as the differentiator.
• AI importance is rated Neutral and the description records no AI adoption currently with prospect curious in an ROI-driven posture. Four meetings on record, including a deep-dive 89-minute session, but the messaging is about building a generic procurement business case rather than an AI-led transformation. With no AI champion, this sits cleanly on the Curious side.
